title: Checkpoint Files, Atomic Replacement, Versioning, and Resume · Program Defense
summary: A release reviewer compares near-miss Go decisions for checkpoint files, atomic replacement, versioning, and resume and accepts only compile and runtime changed-case evidence, explicit non-claims, and named network, DNS, parser, browser, accessibility, security, load, legal-review, and production transfer gates. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for crash-consistent policy-bound checkpoint. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practic…
objectives: Choose policy, seed, frontier, attempts, digests, records, reports, and counters that must survive versus recomputable state.
Use explicit portable schema, size limits, checksums, compatibility rules, and unknown-field policy rather than implicit struct layout.
Write temporary state, flush and sync under declared durability, validate, rename atomically, sync directory where required, and retain fallback.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and Go Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Offline Resume Audit
steps.instruction:
quiz case — A quiz Go team handles case 7865 during a malformed HTML and parser differential clinic. Build deterministic Go evidence to prove durable-state boundaries, versioned formats, atomic replacement, in-flight recovery, and offline audit; every body, file, timer, transport, goroutine, channel, collector, and browser-service request needs one owner; then change one content version, metadata, accessibility, resource, browser-service, or report condition without overclaiming. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser-service, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, x/net/html, goquery, Colly, files, processes, race, load, assistive-technology review, containers, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Load and reconcile checkpoints without network effects, require current authorization, then prove no lost or duplicated state before restart.

The visible result looks correct, but its invariant or value contract is unverified. What must happen next?
